page: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server runs at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and features a 40 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is its substantial local storage capacity of 5765 GB, which is Premium Storage capable, while it lacks any onboard GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the poor to weak categories, reflecting the lower 2.0 GHz clock speed of the older Intel Xeon v3 architecture. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, consistently reach average levels. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also align with average tier performance, as do database operations and Redis SET throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L32s offers high resource density, particularly regarding its massive 5.7 TB local storage and 256 GB memory footprint relative to its compute resources. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for storage-heavy workloads that do not require cutting-edge single-thread performance. The server is best suited for large-scale databases, big data analytics, and storage-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from high local capacity and balanced multi-threaded processing.
description: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 256.0 GB of RAM and a massive 5765 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage, with no GPU resources. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU and text processing speeds are weak, whereas multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. Qualitatively, the server provides high storage and memory density relative to its compute profile. It is highly suited for storage-intensive workloads, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that leverage its large local disk capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and 5765 GB of local storage, optimized for storage-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32s is an LS family virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and 5765 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual machine featuring massive local capacity and balanced multi-core performance for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
256 GB of system memory providing 8 GB of RAM per core
5765 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Weak single-core performance offset by high storage and memory density
Best suited for large-scale databases and storage-intensive applications
